tossups.question: One of these buildings features the figures of “Manufacture” and “Commerce” flanking the crowned central figure on a pediment designed by William Calder Marshall. One of these buildings in Leeds designed by Cuthbert Brodrick was the tallest building in the city until 1996. “The Great Abel” is situated on another one of these buildings. This was the original function of a building nicknamed the “Glass (*) Gonad” until it was superseded by a repurposed exhibition centre dubbed “The Crystal”. Twelve murals by Ford Madox Brown are featured in one of these buildings in Manchester that was designed by Alfred Waterhouse and is the tallest of these buildings in the UK. For 10 points, name these buildings that serve as the administrative headquarters of municipalities.
tossups.question_sanitized: One of these buildings features the figures of "Manufacture" and "Commerce" flanking the crowned central figure on a pediment designed by William Calder Marshall. One of these buildings in Leeds designed by Cuthbert Brodrick was the tallest building in the city until 1996. "The Great Abel" is situated on another one of these buildings. This was the original function of a building nicknamed the "Glass (*) Gonad" until it was superseded by a repurposed exhibition centre dubbed "The Crystal". Twelve murals by Ford Madox Brown are featured in one of these buildings in Manchester that was designed by Alfred Waterhouse and is the tallest of these buildings in the UK. For 10 points, name these buildings that serve as the administrative headquarters of municipalities.
tossups.answer: town halls [or city halls; accept civic centres; prompt on clock tower by asking “what larger structure was the tower a part of?”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: town halls [or city halls; accept civic centres; prompt on clock tower by asking "what larger structure was the tower a part of?"]

tossups.question: This painting’s artist wrote that its centermost figure “occupies the place of the hero” in borrowing a pose from the Apollo Belvedere, in contrast to a barefoot “ragged wretch who has never been taught” about this painting’s title concept. A vignette in the right background of this painting shows campaigners in red sandwich boards reading “Vote for Bobus.” In the foreground of this painting, a girl in an oversized garnet dress with her back to the viewer holds a baby as she reaches over to hit her mischievous younger brother. The elegant lady with a parasol and another handing out temperance pamphlets on this painting’s left contrast with the dirty flower seller positioned in front of them. On the right of this painting, Frederick Maurice and Thomas Carlyle watch as a trio of navvies digs up a road. For 10 points, name this masterpiece of Ford Madox Brown that shows various Victorians toiling.
tossups.question_sanitized: This painting's artist wrote that its centermost figure "occupies the place of the hero" in borrowing a pose from the Apollo Belvedere, in contrast to a barefoot "ragged wretch who has never been taught" about this painting's title concept. A vignette in the right background of this painting shows campaigners in red sandwich boards reading "Vote for Bobus." In the foreground of this painting, a girl in an oversized garnet dress with her back to the viewer holds a baby as she reaches over to hit her mischievous younger brother. The elegant lady with a parasol and another handing out temperance pamphlets on this painting's left contrast with the dirty flower seller positioned in front of them. On the right of this painting, Frederick Maurice and Thomas Carlyle watch as a trio of navvies digs up a road. For 10 points, name this masterpiece of Ford Madox Brown that shows various Victorians toiling.

tossups.question: In 1856, the artist Francis Oliphant published a plea for work in this medium. Ford Madox Brown designed a work in this medium showing King René's Honeymoon. A series of works in this medium depicting the story of Tristram and Isolde were designed by (*) a team of artists including Dante Gabriel Rossetti and William Morris. Augustus Pugin's The Temptation of Eve is a work in this medium in West Tofts, Norfolk. For 10 points, name this medium, works in which by Edward Burne-Jones can be found in the windows of ecclesiastical buildings nationwide.
tossups.answer_sanitized: stained glass [accept painted glass; prompt on glass]
tossups.question_sanitized: In 1856, the artist Francis Oliphant published a plea for work in this medium. Ford Madox Brown designed a work in this medium showing King Rene's Honeymoon. A series of works in this medium depicting the story of Tristram and Isolde were designed by (*) a team of artists including Dante Gabriel Rossetti and William Morris. Augustus Pugin's The Temptation of Eve is a work in this medium in West Tofts, Norfolk. For 10 points, name this medium, works in which by Edward Burne-Jones can be found in the windows of ecclesiastical buildings nationwide.

tossups.question: The artist of this work claimed to have painted it at a point when he was “very hard up and a little mad” and was considering performing the action depicted in this painting. In the background of this painting, a toddler and a brown-gloved hand grasp a scarf below a figure smoking a clay pipe. The rightmost of this painting’s two central figures holds the hands of both her husband and her otherwise-invisible baby while sheltering under a (*) black umbrella. In this painting, a paddle-steamer is visible in front of the White Cliffs of Dover. An emigrant couple turn away from the title country of, for 10 points, what painting by Ford Madox Brown?
tossups.question_sanitized: The artist of this work claimed to have painted it at a point when he was "very hard up and a little mad" and was considering performing the action depicted in this painting. In the background of this painting, a toddler and a brown-gloved hand grasp a scarf below a figure smoking a clay pipe. The rightmost of this painting's two central figures holds the hands of both her husband and her otherwise-invisible baby while sheltering under a (*) black umbrella. In this painting, a paddle-steamer is visible in front of the White Cliffs of Dover. An emigrant couple turn away from the title country of, for 10 points, what painting by Ford Madox Brown?

tossups.question: A copy of The Times can be seen under the arm of a beer seller in this painting, in the foreground of which is a young girl holding a baby in her left arm and holding a boy by the ear with her right hand. Reverend Frederick Maurice and Thomas Carlyle appear in the right-hand side of this painting, in the centre of which has labourers shovelling quicklime and earth. For 10 points, name this Ford Madox Brown painting that depicts the 19th Century transition from a rural to an urban economy.

packet.name: ROUND 1 SJC - Positive Aspect +Teitler + Broken Hearts B

tossups.question_sanitized: A copy of The Times can be seen under the arm of a beer seller in this painting, in the foreground of which is a young girl holding a baby in her left arm and holding a boy by the ear with her right hand. Reverend Frederick Maurice and Thomas Carlyle appear in the right-hand side of this painting, in the centre of which has labourers shovelling quicklime and earth. For 10 points, name this Ford Madox Brown painting that depicts the 19th Century transition from a rural to an urban economy.

bonuses.leadin: An unfinished portrait of this man by John Everett Millais was attacked by suffragette Anne Hunt in protest of Emmeline Pankhurst’s arrest. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this man who was depicted next to fellow thinker F. D. Maurice in Ford Madox Brown’s painting Work. The painting also references Bobus, a corrupt politician created by this man.
Thomas Carlyle’s writings were influential to members of this movement, which John Everett Millais and Ford Madox Brown were associated with.
Thomas Carlyle’s influence on the PRB also includes being one of many models that William Holman Hunt used in this painting, depicting Jesus in a crown and lavish green robes knocking on a door.

bonuses.leadin: Thomas Carlyle and Frederick Maurice watch laborers dig up a street in this painting. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painting by Ford Madox Brown that depicts "navvies" building an underground tunnel in London.
Brown's painting The Last of England is this type of painting, characterized by its circular shape. Another example of this type of painting is Fra Angelico's Adoration of the Magi.
Brown was associated with this British art movement that sought to reject the influence of Mannerism in painting. Members of this group included Dante Gabriel Rossetti and William Holman Hunt.
